article_meta.title: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell on June 25, 2025, in Washington, DC

reasoning: The summary avoids the article’s loaded framing and attributes evaluative claims to sources. It uses mostly neutral language, with minor interpretive words like “rebuked” and “forceful,” and includes both supportive and critical perspectives present in the article.

custom_reasoning.spin: Mostly neutral verbs; “rebuked” and “forceful” are mildly interpretive but limited.
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: States “legal experts” broadly when only one is quoted; otherwise attributes claims and notes allegations.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Minimal; one phrase (“first forceful public opposition”) is mildly evaluative but consistent with article context.
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No dramatic or alarmist language; toned down compared to source.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No personal attacks by the model.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No claims about internal motives beyond attributed quotes.
custom_reasoning.slant: Includes support for Powell and mentions Trump allies’ accusations; emphasis roughly mirrors the source.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Main perspectives (Powell, Trump allies, legal expert, international central bankers, some Republicans) are present.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Avoids politically charged labels; uses neutral terminology with attributions.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Balanced tone consistent with source content.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: Treats official and expert sources descriptively without privileging unjustifiably.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: Substantially more neutral than the opinion piece; loaded claims are attributed to sources.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Captures the key sides proportionally; slight under-detail on Trump’s broader rationale but not a major omission.
